description: Outbox coalescing keeps the unacked cursor-queue bounded under a persistently-slow (non-acking) peer WITHOUT dropping an op (protocol.md § Backpressure & outbox coalescing, #lzsync-backpressure). The unacked outbox is a bounded cursor-queue: append pushes the tail, OutboxAck.through_epoch dequeues the acked front (FIFO), retained depth is the fill level. How the suffix coalesces is dispatched by the element's merge algebra: a state cell (LWW → last value, OR-set → union, graph → Snapshot) has an idempotent join and collapses its suffix to one frame (memory-bounded, ReliableSync.coalesce_by_join_sound / coalesce_to_snapshot_state_equiv); an op-log cell (QueueCell) has no idempotent join and only fuses a run of same-direction ops into one batch (frame-bounded, order-preserving, ReliableSync.batch_fusion_state). Coalescing is a retention optimization only: the receiver observes identical effects. Non-ack IS backpressure; liveness cells disambiguate slow (fill, wait) from dead (drain, reconnect+replay).

scenarios.name: state_suffix_collapses_to_snapshot
scenarios.description: Sender appends deltas at epochs 1,2,3 (graph/state frames); the peer is stalled at last_epoch=0 and never acks, so retained depth is 3. At the host watermark the outbox coalesces the unacked suffix (1..3) into one Snapshot at epoch 3 carrying the merged state — retained depth drops to 1. A receiver at last_epoch=0 that adopts that Snapshot reaches the same graph and last_epoch=3 as a receiver that applied all three deltas. Memory bounded to one frame regardless of how far the peer is behind.

scenarios.name: oplog_declines_snapshot_fuses_batch
scenarios.description: A QueueCell (op-log) outbox holds a run of same-direction push ops at epochs 1,2,3 (each queue element materializes as a distinct cell op — nodes 7,8,9 — so the run is append-like, not same-node overwrite; the concrete op-log frame encoding is deferred to the op-log frame spec). It DECLINES snapshot-coalesce (coalesce_to_snapshot returns false — a queue snapshot would discard the receiver's independent consumption position). Instead it fuses the run into one multi-epoch batch Delta (base_epoch 0 → epoch 3) carrying the ops in order. Lossless and order-preserving: the fused batch applies equal to the three unit deltas (multi_epoch_apply_eq_fold / batch_fusion_state). Frame count drops 3 → 1; retained PAYLOAD volume is unchanged (a queue cannot drop elements), so memory is still bounded by source-side QueueCell.is_full, not by this fusion.

scenarios.name: non_ack_fills_then_ack_drains_cursor_queue
scenarios.description: Backpressure as a queue property. With the peer not acking, three appends leave retained depth 3 (queue fills toward is_full). An OutboxAck { through_epoch: 2 } dequeues the acked front (epochs 1,2) FIFO, leaving only epoch 3 — retained depth 1 (queue drains below watermark; is_full clears; producer may resume). Formalized ReliableSync.outbox_is_bounded_queue (enqueue grows depth by one; ack dequeues the front; coalesced suffix is one frame).
